0.label: LLM Reliability and Hallucination Mitigation
0.description: Research on detecting, understanding, and mitigating hallucinations, miscalibration, and unreliable outputs in large language models and vision-language models.
subtopics: Hallucination detection and mitigation in LLMs and multimodal models
Uncertainty quantification and calibration for language models
Factual consistency evaluation and fact-checking
Attention mechanisms and internal representations driving hallucinations
Training and decoding strategies for improving model reliability

1.label: Face Forgery Detection and Privacy
1.description: Research on detecting manipulated facial imagery (deepfakes) and preserving visual privacy in face-centric computer vision systems.
subtopics: Deepfake detection and generalization across unseen generators
Privacy-preserving face and activity recognition
Image and video manipulation localization
Datasets and benchmarks for forgery and manipulation
Face generation, morphing, and de-identification techniques

2.label: Adversarial Robustness in Deep Learning
2.description: Research on adversarial attacks against and defenses for deep neural networks across vision, language, and multimodal models, encompassing attack generation, robustness evaluation, and defense mechanisms.
subtopics: Adversarial attack methods and transferability
Adversarial training and defense strategies
Backdoor attacks and detection in multimodal models
Adversarial robustness of large vision-language models
Watermarking and copyright protection against adversarial manipulation

3.label: Causal Inference and Discovery Methods
3.description: Research advancing causal inference, causal discovery, and causal reasoning methods across diverse domains using statistical, machine learning, and large language model approaches.
subtopics: Causal discovery algorithms and graphical models
Treatment effect estimation and meta-learners
Causal reasoning with large language models
Causal inference from time-series and observational data
Applied causal machine learning in real-world domains

4.label: Federated Learning Privacy and Efficiency
4.description: Research on federated learning systems addressing communication efficiency, data heterogeneity, privacy preservation, and security against adversarial attacks in distributed machine learning.
subtopics: Communication efficiency and compression in federated learning
Handling data heterogeneity and non-IID distributions
Privacy attacks and defenses (membership inference, data reconstruction, backdoors)
Personalized and model-heterogeneous federated learning
Federated fine-tuning of large models with parameter-efficient methods

5.label: Graph Neural Networks Research Advances
5.description: Research on graph neural networks and graph-structured data modeling, spanning architecture design, learning paradigms, and diverse applications such as node classification, link prediction, and integration with large language models.
subtopics: GNN architectures and expressiveness (message passing, metapaths, latent graph inference)
Integration of LLMs with graph-structured data
Graph augmentation and domain generalization techniques
Temporal and dynamic graph modeling
GNN explainability, privacy, and robustness

6.label: Time Series Forecasting and Analysis
6.description: Research on advanced deep learning architectures and foundation models for time series forecasting, classification, anomaly detection, and reasoning across diverse application domains.
subtopics: Foundation models and LLMs for time series forecasting and reasoning
Transformer and Mamba architectures for multivariate time series
Pre-training, transfer learning, and zero/few-shot strategies for time series
Time series representation learning, clustering, and classification
Domain-specific applications (energy, healthcare, supply chain, process monitoring)

7.label: AI for Molecular and Biological Discovery
7.description: This cluster encompasses research applying machine learning and deep learning methods—including language models, graph neural networks, diffusion models, and contrastive learning—to molecular, protein, and biological structure prediction, generation, and property optimization.
subtopics: Molecular generation and representation learning (SMILES, neural fields, diffusion models)
Protein structure, function, and interaction prediction
Drug discovery and repositioning
Biological sequence modeling with transformers
Materials and crystal property prediction

8.label: Remote Sensing and Environmental AI
8.description: AI and deep learning methods applied to remote sensing, environmental monitoring, agriculture, and ecological analysis across diverse imaging modalities.
subtopics: Hyperspectral image processing and classification
Underwater image enhancement and detection
Agricultural disease detection and crop monitoring
Land use/land cover mapping and segmentation
Multi-modal and spectral data fusion for environmental tasks

9.label: Optimization Algorithms and Metaheuristics
9.description: Research on advancing optimization methods—including evolutionary algorithms, Bayesian optimization, and LLM-assisted approaches—for solving complex, multi-objective, and large-scale optimization problems.
subtopics: LLM-assisted optimization and algorithm design
Multi-objective and Pareto optimization
Bayesian optimization and surrogate modeling
Evolutionary and swarm intelligence algorithms
Combinatorial optimization and scheduling

10.label: Visual-LiDAR Localization and Place Recognition
10.description: Research on robotic localization, place recognition, and SLAM systems leveraging visual, LiDAR, and multi-modal sensor data for autonomous navigation and mapping.
subtopics: Visual place recognition and feature aggregation methods
LiDAR-based odometry, mapping, and scan matching
Multi-modal and cross-modal sensor fusion for localization
SLAM systems (monocular, RGB-D, Gaussian splatting, NeRF-based)
Datasets and benchmarks for localization and mapping evaluation

11.label: Emotion Recognition and Sentiment Analysis
11.description: Research on detecting, classifying, and understanding emotions and sentiments across multiple modalities, languages, and application domains using modern AI techniques.
subtopics: Multimodal emotion recognition (visual, speech, physiological, text)
Multilingual and cross-lingual sentiment and emotion detection
Large language models for emotion cognition and sentiment analysis
Emotion datasets, benchmarks, and evaluation frameworks
Emotion-driven creative and interactive applications

12.label: Anomaly Detection Methods and Applications
12.description: Research on novel algorithms and frameworks for detecting anomalies across diverse domains including video surveillance, industrial manufacturing, medical imaging, cybersecurity, and tabular data.
subtopics: Video anomaly detection using multi-modal and deep learning approaches
Industrial visual defect detection and quality inspection
Unsupervised and few-shot anomaly detection with foundation models
Graph-based and tree-based anomaly detection algorithms
Medical and 3D anomaly detection frameworks

13.label: Physics-Informed ML for Engineering
13.description: Research at the intersection of physics-informed machine learning, neural operators, and deep learning applied to modeling physical systems, solving PDEs, and engineering condition monitoring.
subtopics: Physics-Informed Neural Networks for PDE solving
Neural operators and surrogate models for dynamical systems
Fault diagnosis and condition monitoring with deep learning
Knowledge distillation and lightweight models for edge deployment
Physics-constrained and geometry-aware learning architectures

14.label: AI for Weather and Climate
14.description: This cluster encompasses research applying machine learning and deep learning techniques to weather forecasting, climate modeling, natural disaster monitoring, and environmental prediction across diverse spatiotemporal scales.
subtopics: Data-driven weather and climate forecasting
Disaster detection, mapping, and response
Explainable and interpretable AI for environmental science
Deep learning for data assimilation and physical parameterization
Energy and infrastructure forecasting under climate variability

15.label: Image Restoration and Enhancement
15.description: This cluster focuses on deep learning methods for restoring and enhancing degraded images, including denoising, super-resolution, dehazing, low-light enhancement, and unified all-in-one restoration frameworks.
subtopics: All-in-one multi-degradation image/video restoration
Image denoising (self-supervised, zero-shot, and CNN-based)
Super-resolution and low-light image enhancement
State space models (Mamba) for vision tasks
Face restoration and perceptual quality assessment

16.label: Explainability Methods for AI Models
16.description: Research on techniques, frameworks, and theoretical foundations for explaining and interpreting predictions made by machine learning models across various architectures and domains.
subtopics: Theoretical foundations of explanations (certificates, complexity, formal verification)
Prototype-based and concept-based interpretable models
Post-hoc explanation methods (attribution, saliency maps, feature importance)
Evaluation metrics and frameworks for explanation quality
Mechanistic interpretability and sparse autoencoders for neural network internals

17.label: Transformer Architecture Analysis and Optimization
17.description: Research on understanding, compressing, and improving the efficiency and interpretability of transformer and state space model architectures for large language models.
subtopics: KV cache compression and efficient inference (sparse attention, quantization, eviction)
Mechanistic interpretability and understanding of transformer internals
Hybrid architectures combining attention and state space models
Model compression through pruning, distillation, and depth optimization
Theoretical analysis of transformer capabilities and training objectives

18.label: Medical AI and Image Segmentation
18.description: Research at the intersection of medical/healthcare AI applications and advanced image segmentation methods, spanning clinical decision support, medical imaging analysis, and vision-language models.
subtopics: Image segmentation methods (few-shot, open-vocabulary, point-prompted)
Medical imaging and computational pathology
Vision-language models for healthcare applications
LLMs and multimodal AI in clinical decision-making
Physiological signal analysis and brain-computer interfaces

19.label: Domain Adaptation and Object Detection
19.description: Research on improving object detection and visual recognition through domain adaptation, generalization, and feature enhancement techniques across diverse imaging conditions.
subtopics: Domain adaptation and generalization methods (source-free, zero-shot, unsupervised)
YOLO-based object detection architectures and improvements
Small object and remote sensing detection
Vision-language models (CLIP, SAM) for transfer learning and adaptation
Multi-scale feature fusion and pyramid network enhancements

20.label: Video Understanding and Retrieval
20.description: Research on multimodal methods for video understanding, retrieval, temporal grounding, and summarization leveraging language models and cross-modal learning.
subtopics: Text-video retrieval and cross-modal alignment
Temporal grounding and event localization in videos
Video summarization and keyframe extraction
Multimodal large language models for video comprehension
Audio-visual learning and segmentation

21.label: Video-Based Human Action Understanding
21.description: Research on recognizing, localizing, and analyzing human actions in videos using deep learning, skeleton data, and multimodal approaches across domains including sports, daily living, and egocentric settings.
subtopics: Skeleton-based action recognition and representation learning
Temporal action localization and segmentation
Sports video analytics and tactical analysis
Multimodal and vision-language models for action understanding
Activity recognition in daily living and smart environments

22.label: Human-Centric 3D Vision and Animation
22.description: Research focused on reconstructing, generating, and animating 3D human bodies, faces, and motions from visual or multimodal inputs, encompassing avatars, pose estimation, motion synthesis, and talking face generation.
subtopics: 3D avatar reconstruction and editing
Human motion generation and synthesis
Talking face generation and facial animation
Human pose estimation and body perception
Music/audio-driven body and gesture generation

23.label: AI Safety and Adversarial Robustness
23.description: Research on evaluating, attacking, and defending AI systems—particularly large language models and vision-language models—against adversarial threats, harmful outputs, and safety alignment failures across diverse modalities and languages.
subtopics: Jailbreak attacks and defenses for LLMs and VLMs
Multilingual and cross-cultural AI safety evaluation
Harmful content detection and moderation (hate speech, toxicity, abuse)
Prompt injection and adversarial manipulation of AI agents
Safety alignment, guardrails, and compliance in deployed AI systems

24.label: Multimodal Vision-Language Models and Adaptation
24.description: Research on advancing multimodal vision-language models through improved fusion, adaptation, reasoning, and handling of missing modalities for diverse downstream tasks.
subtopics: Missing modality handling and robust multimodal fusion
Vision-language model efficiency, distillation, and prompt tuning
Multimodal retrieval, grounding, and document understanding
Interpretability and reasoning in vision-language models
Benchmarks and evaluation for multimodal tasks

25.label: Uncertainty Quantification and Calibration Methods
25.description: Research on improving uncertainty estimation, prediction calibration, and confidence quantification in deep learning models across classification, self-supervised learning, and conformal prediction frameworks.
subtopics: Conformal prediction training and risk control
Model calibration and epistemic uncertainty estimation
Self-supervised and contrastive representation learning
Learning under label noise and domain shift
Feature calibration and single-pass uncertainty methods

26.label: Diffusion Model Advances and Optimization
26.description: Research on improving diffusion-based generative models through acceleration, distillation, architectural innovations, training strategies, and controlled generation techniques.
subtopics: Inference acceleration and step reduction (caching, token pruning, adaptive step skipping)
One-step and few-step distillation methods (flow matching, divergence-based frameworks)
Conditional and controlled image generation (spatial control, style transfer, safety constraints)
Fine-tuning and training improvements (self-play, Bayesian steering, regularization losses)
Understanding and analysis of diffusion models (bias-variance tradeoff, semantic similarity, incompleteness)

27.label: Video Generation and Processing
27.description: Research advancing video generation, editing, compression, and control using diffusion models and neural representations, with emphasis on temporal coherence, controllability, and quality assessment.
subtopics: Controllable video generation (motion, camera, layout)
Video quality and temporal coherence enhancement
Video editing and customization with diffusion models
Neural video compression and representation
Video generation evaluation and benchmarking

28.label: Neural Network Training and Optimization
28.description: This cluster explores advances in neural network architectures, training algorithms, optimization methods, and their theoretical foundations, spanning deep learning, spiking neural networks, and efficient computation.
subtopics: Optimization algorithms for deep learning (Adam variants, second-order methods, zero-order methods)
Spiking neural networks for energy-efficient inference and learning
Generalization, stability, and implicit bias in neural network training
Novel neural network architectures (KANs, task-based neurons, NAS)
Training dynamics and loss landscape analysis (neural collapse, sharpness, symmetry)

29.label: Neural Network Compression and Quantization
29.description: Research on techniques for compressing and quantizing neural networks to improve deployment efficiency while preserving model accuracy.
subtopics: Post-training and quantization-aware training methods for LLMs and vision models
Network pruning strategies including depth, width, and channel pruning
Theoretical foundations of quantization and lossless compression
Efficient deployment on edge and resource-constrained devices
Quantization of generative models including diffusion and language-style architectures

30.label: Multilingual NLP and Speech Processing
30.description: Research on developing, evaluating, and improving natural language processing and speech recognition systems across diverse languages, with emphasis on multilingual models, low-resource languages, and cross-lingual transfer.
subtopics: Low-resource and African language NLP tools and benchmarks
Multilingual and code-switching automatic speech recognition
Cross-lingual text classification and topic modeling
Large language models for multilingual and Indic language support
Machine translation and speech translation systems

31.label: LLM Evaluation and Educational Applications
31.description: Research on evaluating large language model performance through benchmarks, automated judging, and applying LLMs in educational contexts such as tutoring, grading, and question generation.
subtopics: LLM-as-judge benchmarking and calibration methods
Automated essay and short-answer grading with LLMs
LLM-based intelligent tutoring systems and pedagogical frameworks
Multiple-choice question answering biases and mechanisms in LLMs
Benchmark design, saturation, and model differentiation metrics

32.label: Reinforcement Learning Methods and Applications
32.description: This cluster encompasses diverse advances in reinforcement learning theory, algorithms, and applications spanning offline/online settings, safety constraints, multi-agent systems, and real-world robotics deployment.
subtopics: Offline and offline-to-online reinforcement learning
Safe and robust reinforcement learning with constraints
Multi-agent reinforcement learning and communication
Real-world robotics deployment and policy learning
Foundational RL theory: value estimation, policy iteration, and reward shaping

33.label: Robot Planning, Control & Manipulation
33.description: This cluster encompasses research on autonomous robot systems spanning motion planning, multi-agent coordination, locomotion control, manipulation, and human-robot interaction across diverse platforms and environments.
subtopics: Multi-agent path finding and trajectory planning
Legged robot locomotion and humanoid control
Robot manipulation and contact-rich interaction
Human-robot interaction and assistive interfaces
Learning-based policies for embodied control

34.label: Recommender Systems Methods and Frameworks
34.description: Research on novel architectures, paradigms, and optimization strategies for improving recommendation system accuracy, personalization, fairness, and scalability across diverse settings.
subtopics: Generative and sequential recommendation models
LLM-enhanced and reasoning-augmented recommendations
Federated and privacy-preserving recommendation
Debiasing, fairness, and counterfactual augmentation
Collaborative filtering and graph-based approaches

35.label: Retrieval-Augmented Generation and Reranking
35.description: Research on improving information retrieval, reranking, and retrieval-augmented generation systems for enhanced question answering and knowledge-grounded language model outputs.
subtopics: Query rewriting and reformulation strategies
Reranking methods using LLMs and reinforcement learning
RAG pipeline architectures and modular design
Context selection, chunking, and metadata enrichment
Multi-hop and conversational question answering with retrieval

36.label: Bias and Fairness in Language Models
36.description: This cluster investigates biases, fairness issues, and framing effects in large language models and NLP systems across social, political, and cultural dimensions.
subtopics: Demographic and social bias detection and benchmarking in LLMs
Bias mitigation and debiasing techniques for language models
Media framing, political bias, and narrative analysis using NLP
Privacy, tone, and format biases in LLM outputs
Cross-cultural and cross-domain generalization of bias frameworks

37.label: LLM Alignment and AI Personality
37.description: Research on aligning large language models with human preferences through preference optimization techniques and evaluating AI personality and persona simulation.
subtopics: Preference optimization methods (DPO, RLHF, and variants)
AI personality evaluation and psychometric assessment
Persona generation and simulation for LLMs
Personalized LLM alignment with diverse user preferences
Reward modeling and preference data synthesis

38.label: Autonomous Driving and Traffic Intelligence
38.description: Research advancing autonomous and connected vehicle systems through perception, prediction, planning, collaboration, and simulation for safer and more efficient transportation.
subtopics: Collaborative and cooperative vehicle perception and communication (V2X, platooning, multi-agent)
Trajectory prediction and motion forecasting for vehicles and pedestrians
Autonomous driving planning and control (path planning, lane change, end-to-end systems)
Traffic simulation, safety evaluation, and crash prediction
LLM and vision-language model applications in driving and mobility

39.label: 3D Point Cloud Perception
39.description: Research on processing, understanding, and generating 3D point cloud and related geometric data for tasks spanning autonomous driving, scene understanding, segmentation, and robustness.
subtopics: Autonomous driving perception (LiDAR, radar, semantic scene completion)
Point cloud classification, segmentation, and registration
3D Gaussian splatting and radiance field segmentation
Adversarial robustness and domain adaptation for 3D data
Point cloud generation, completion, and representation learning

40.label: 3D Scene Reconstruction and Rendering
40.description: Research on neural and Gaussian-based 3D scene representation, reconstruction, and novel view synthesis with emphasis on quality, efficiency, and generation.
subtopics: 3D Gaussian Splatting optimization and compression
Neural implicit surface reconstruction (SDF/NeRF)
Text/image-to-3D generation and texturing
Camera pose estimation and multi-view consistency
Large-scale and indoor scene reconstruction

41.label: LLM Agents and Decision-Making Systems
41.description: Research on leveraging large language models as autonomous agents for complex reasoning, tool use, multi-agent coordination, and decision-making across scientific, social, and interactive domains.
subtopics: Tool use and agentic workflow optimization
Multi-agent systems and cooperative decision-making
LLM agents for scientific discovery and simulation
Search, planning, and reasoning in interactive environments
Benchmarking and evaluating LLM agent capabilities

42.label: LLM Reasoning Optimization Methods
42.description: Research on improving the reasoning capabilities and efficiency of large language models through reinforcement learning, structured prompting, and adaptive inference-time strategies.
subtopics: Reinforcement learning algorithms for LLM reasoning (GRPO, PPO variants, reward design)
Adaptive and efficient reasoning strategies (token efficiency, over-thinking mitigation, test-time scaling)
Structured reasoning frameworks (Chain-of-Thought, Tree-of-Thought, recursive reasoning)
Domain-specific and formal reasoning (theorem proving, legal, logical reasoning)
Knowledge distillation and curriculum learning for reasoning
